channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Everything new on Disney+ in April 2025: Andor season 2, Doctor Who season 15, Dying for Sex, and more
item.description: From the return of hugely popular shows to new movies, here's what you'll want to watch on Disney+ in April.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: North Korea's suicide drone test
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Revolutionizing Healthcare: 3D Telemedicine Technology Brings Hope to Ghana's Patients
item.description: A new trial in Ghana is using 3D telemedicine technology to provide medical consultations for patients awaiting surgery or after an operation, aiming to address the country's low doctor-to-patient ratio.
item.dc:creator: Naa Adorkor Cudjoe, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google introduces Gemini 2.5 reasoning models
item.description: Google has introduced version 2.5 of its Gemini AI model, which the company said offers a new level of performance by combining an enhanced base model with improved post-training.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: ECMAScript 2025 JavaScript standard takes shape
item.description: ECMAScript 2025, the next version of an ECMA International standard for JavaScript, will standardize new JavaScript capabilities ranging from JSON modules to import attributes, new Set methods, sync iterator helpers, and regular expression modifiers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What to know about earthquakes like the one that hit Myanmar
item.description: A major 7.7 magnitude earthquake that originated near Mandalay, Myanmar shook the Earth as far as Bangkok.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: This may be the most private and secure cloud-based AI solution around
item.description: What if you could build a business-specific generative AI (genAI) solution that was hosted in the cloud? What if that cloud used the most energy-efficient systems in the world? And what if those services were also all running on the world’s most secure computing platform, the Mac? Wonder no more, b
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New Variant of PJobRAT Targets Taiwanese Users - Android RAT Capabilities Expanded
item.description: Sophos researchers discover new variant of PJobRAT targeting Taiwanese users with increased capabilities and functionalities, including running shell commands and exfiltrating data.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Modernize enterprise PC fleets to ensure AI ambitions deliver business success
item.description: Investing in the right kind of PCs helps businesses innovate, become more secure and adapt to new ways of working. They also ensure that enterprises have a foundation from which to implement crucial AI-driven improvements that can boost productivity and growth.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Firefox patches zero-day security flaw days after Chrome fixes the same issue
item.description: A bug similar to one recently found in Google Chrome was also spotted in Firefox and subsequently fixed.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Hang on handheld fans, we could be seeing AMD's FSR 4 upscaling on new handhelds after all - if this new RDNA 4 leak is legitimate
item.description: It's time to get excited again about the future of handheld gaming PCs, because a new leak suggests FSR 4 could be making its way to Medusa Point-powered devices.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple, Meta face modest fines as EU seeks to avoid US spat
item.description: Apple Inc. and Meta Platforms Inc. are set to be hit by fines under the European Union's Big Tech competition rulebook toward the end of next week, with the iPhone maker facing the added threat of recurring penalties under the bloc's Digital Markets Act.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: A future Apple Watch could debut a glass frame with touch controls
item.description: A new leak and patent indicate that Apple is working on a future Apple Watch with a touchscreen glass frame.
item.dc:creator: stephen.warwick@futurenet.com (Stephen Warwick), TechRadar

item.title: Xiaomi's Google TV Streamer rival gets a new processor and a much-needed storage leap
item.description: The 3rd-gen Xiaomi TV Box S is now official, though we're still waiting on price and availability details.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Convicted of bilking investors, Nikola founder and Trump donor gets a presidential pardon
item.description: The founder of an electric car start-up sentenced to prison for exaggerating the potential of his technology has been pardoned by President Donald Trump, potentially wiping out hundreds of millions of dollars in restitution that prosecutors were seeking for bamboozled investors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What is 'classified' information? What are 'secure' communications? Here's a primer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's use of a popular messaging app to discuss sensitive military plans — with a journalist on the text chain — is raising questions about security and the importance of safeguarding the nation's secrets.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: An AI pioneer is launching a blockchain-powered marketplace for open-source models and applications
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Adobe announces AI agents for customer interaction
item.description: Adobe has announced Adobe Experience Platform Agent Orchestrator, an intelligent reasoning engine that allows AI agents to perform complex decision-making and problem-solving tasks to accelerate customer experience orchestration.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Los Angeles sheriff's department to retest 4,000 DNA samples after using faulty kits for 8 months
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles County Sheriff’s Department said this week it will retest 4,000 DNA samples and open an internal investigation after learning that it used potentially flawed test kits for eight months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Figure skating embraces the future with new technology, modern concepts to engage athletes and fans
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ashley Wagner and Ben Agosto know what it's like to step off the ice at the figure skating world championships and feel as though they've conquered the sport. And what it feels like when the notoriously fickle sport bites back.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The secret to using generative AI effectively
item.description: Do you think generative AI (genAI) sucks? I did. The hype around everything genAI has been over the top and ridiculous for a while now. Especially at the start, most of the tools were flashy, but quickly fell apart if you tried to use them for any serious work purposes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Striking the balance between information sharing and security
item.description: That balancing act requires a complex response, especially when designing networks and systems for collaboration. The post Striking the balance between information sharing and security first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Waymo plans to bring its driverless taxis to Washington in 2026
item.description: Waymo on Tuesday added Washington to its pioneering robotaxi service's steadily expanding list of U.S. markets, although passengers will have to wait until next year until they can take a driverless ride around the nation's capital city.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Scary AI-powered swarm robots team up to build cars faster than ever
item.description: UBTech and Zeekr unite with AI robot swarms to make car manufacturing faster and smarter. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how the process works.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
